subject: Anthropology
title: The Future of Human Evolution
description: Learn The Future of Human Evolution in one minute.

hook.text: Medicine saves lives that evolution would have ended. Gene editing can rewrite DNA. Are we still evolving?

buildup.text: Natural selection still operates — but culture, medicine, and technology now buffer its effects significantly.

discovery.text: CRISPR gene editing could one day eliminate genetic diseases — or enhance traits, raising deep ethical questions.

twist.text: Sexual selection, genetic drift, and gene flow still shape us. Evolution hasn't stopped — it's just changed pace.

climax.text: For the first time, a species can direct its own evolution. The question is whether we should.

punchline.text: We now hold the pen that writes our genes.

quiz.question: Has human evolution stopped?
options: No, but culture and technology have changed its pace
Evolution only affects other species now
